# eXcalibur Editorial

> A composer for X (Twitter) posts, monetized per call in Bitcoin Lightning.
> Write block-structured drafts, refine flagged passages with Claude
> server-side, schedule and repost, and keep a reusable snippet library —
> then publish to X. Identity is a Nostr keypair (npub); usage is metered in
> satoshis — no account, no email, no subscription, no KYC.

eXcalibur is a single-page web app backed by an MCP (Model Context Protocol)
server. Humans use the web UI; AI agents can call the same MCP tools directly.
The operator holds the X API credentials in an encrypted vault; patrons pay
per published post rather than supplying their own keys.

## What is on this site

- **Composer** — a block editor for X posts. Each block is a line/paragraph
  you can reorder; X has no rich text, so "bold/italic/monospace" render as
  Unicode Mathematical Alphanumeric Symbols, dividers as box-drawing rules,
  and an emoji/symbol picker inserts glyphs at the cursor. What you see is
  what posts.
- **Refine with Claude** — flag a region of a draft and the server rewrites
  it against your voice profile and banned constructions. The operator's
  Anthropic key stays vaulted and never reaches the browser; the AI cost is
  metered as a Lightning fare.
- **Posts** — drafts, scheduled, sent, and archived. Repost a sent post in
  one click.
- **Snippet library** — save reusable openings, footers, and CTAs under your
  npub (Neon-backed, follows you across devices); star favorites to get
  one-click chiclets in the editor.
- **Wallet** — pre-fund and check your satoshi balance; preview any tool's
  price before committing.
- **Profile** — display name, avatar, and Nostr relays stored in your Nostr
  kind-0 metadata; theme selection (dark by default); build, license, patent,
  and version transparency.

## Payments and identity

- Sign in with a Nostr npub and prove ownership via a Nostr DM challenge
  (`request_npub_proof` / `receive_npub_proof`), or paste an nsec for an
  instant in-browser session key that signs each call inline.
- Pre-fund a satoshi balance with a Bitcoin Lightning invoice
  (`purchase_credits`); tool calls debit it. `check_price` previews any
  tool's cost before committing.
- Monetization is the Tollbooth DPYC protocol (https://tollbooth-dpyc.com):
  operator-controlled dynamic per-tool pricing at the MCP layer.

## For AI agents

- MCP endpoint: https://excalibur-mcp.fastmcp.app/mcp (streamable HTTP)
- Start with `excalibur_service_status` (free, no arguments) to check
  liveness, then `excalibur_check_price` to preview costs.
- Posting tools: `excalibur_post_tweet`, `excalibur_post_tweet_image`.
- Stored-post CRUD: `excalibur_create_post`, `excalibur_get_post`,
  `excalibur_list_posts`, `excalibur_update_post`, `excalibur_delete_post`.
- Snippet library (free, proof-gated, owner-scoped): `excalibur_list_snippets`,
  `excalibur_save_snippet`, `excalibur_delete_snippet`.
- Server-side AI refine (metered): `excalibur_refine_post_region`.
- Balance and credits (free): `excalibur_check_balance`,
  `excalibur_purchase_credits`, `excalibur_check_payment`.

## License, patent, and trademarks

- Source is open under the Apache License 2.0. The services on top are private
  commerce: each operator sets their own tolls.
- Patent Pending — US Provisional Application 64/045,999 (Tollbooth DPYC
  Economic Architecture).
- DPYC, Tollbooth DPYC, and Don't Pester Your Customer are trademarks of
  Lonnie VanZandt.

## Links

- Web app: https://excalibur-app.pages.dev
- MCP endpoint: https://excalibur-mcp.fastmcp.app/mcp
- Source: https://github.com/lonniev/excalibur-mcp
- Tollbooth DPYC (monetization SDK and network): https://tollbooth-dpyc.com
- DPYC community & patent filings: https://github.com/lonniev/dpyc-community
- DPYC Oracle (free community concierge): https://dpyc-oracle.fastmcp.app/mcp
